title: coalisina semitetrandra
intro: Coalisina semitetrandra, also known as the "Kadupul flower" or "Queen of the night," is a rare and exotic plant native to Sri Lanka, characterized by its unique ability to bloom only at night and wilt by dawn. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Coalisina semitetrandra include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and possess anti-cancer properties, although further research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
